metadata.seed: During the past 2.5 years I have been sober, my mentor along the way turned into my very best friend and now the love of my life...but I am married, and so is she...and I have absolutely no one i can talk to now about this.

patient.query: During the past 2.5 years I have been sober, my mentor along the way turned into my very best friend and now the love of my life...but I am married, and so is she...and I have absolutely no one i can talk to now about this.

automatic_thoughts: I have no one to talk to
this situation is complicated
I can't act on my feelings

patient.query: I guess it feels really heavy to think about—like, I used to have people I could reach out to, but now it just feels... empty. I don’t know, maybe I've pushed them away? It’s confusing.

automatic_thoughts: I used to have people to reach out to
maybe I've pushed them away
it's confusing

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just, I keep thinking about times when I maybe said the wrong thing or didn’t show up when I should have. I guess I worry that they see me as unreliable now, and that makes it even harder to reach out.

automatic_thoughts: I said the wrong thing
I didn't show up
they see me as unreliable

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: I mean, I get that it’s natural to feel this way, but sometimes it feels like it just sticks with me, you know? Like I replay those moments over and over, and it’s hard to see another side. I just... wish I could feel more secure in those friendships.

hard to see another side
wish I could feel more secure

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, I know it’s not all black and white, but it can be so overwhelming, especially when I think about reaching out again. What if they really don't want to hear from me? It just makes my chest tight.

automatic_thoughts: what if they really don't want to hear from me

patient.query: I appreciate that. It’s just hard to shake that feeling of rejection. Sometimes I wonder if they’d even notice if I just... faded away. I don’t know, it feels like I’m on this edge, and I don’t really know how to step back from it.

patient.query: It’s hard to pinpoint one specific moment, but there are times when I reach out, and it feels... quiet. Like, I can text and text, but the replies are sparse, or I’ll see them hanging out without me. It just hits this nerve, I guess. It makes me doubt if I ever really mattered to them, you know?

triggers: seeing them hanging out without me
automatic_thoughts: I doubt if I ever really mattered to them

patient.query: I mean, it definitely feels like there’s a lot of sadness there. When I see them together or I don’t hear back, it’s like this little voice in my head saying I’m not worth their time. I don’t want to feel this way, but it just keeps creeping in. I guess I’m scared of what that means for me.

I'm scared of what that means for me

that’s the only conclusion I can come to

patient.query: I guess it comes from those moments when I see my friends not inviting me out or just... carrying on without me. It’s like I’m on the outside looking in, and I start thinking maybe I didn’t matter as much as I thought. It’s like this loop in my head, and I can’t seem to break out of it.
